Title: Login with valid credentials and select a session location
Preconditions: Provisioned user with role Clinician exists and is enabled; at least one Login Location (Outpatient Clinic) is defined; user is on the OpenMRS login page
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username | 2. Enter the correct password | 3. Select the session location Outpatient Clinic | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: User is authenticated, redirected to the Home dashboard, and the active session location header shows Outpatient Clinic

Title: Login fails with a valid username and an incorrect password
Preconditions: Provisioned user clinician1 exists and is enabled; user is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username | 2. Enter an incorrect password | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is rejected, an Invalid username or password message is shown, the user remains on the login page, and no session is created

Title: Login fails with a non-existent username
Preconditions: No user named ghostuser exists; user is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a non-existent username | 2. Enter any password | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is rejected with a generic Invalid username or password message that does not reveal whether the username exists

Title: Login blocked with empty username field
Preconditions: User is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Leave the username field empty | 2. Enter a valid password | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is not submitted; a required-field validation indicates the username is required and no authentication request is sent

Title: Login blocked with empty password field
Preconditions: User is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username | 2. Leave the password field empty | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is not submitted; a required-field validation indicates the password is required and no authentication request is sent

Title: Login blocked when both username and password are empty
Preconditions: User is on the login page
Test_Steps: 1. Leave username empty | 2. Leave password empty | 3. Select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: Login is not submitted and validation messages for both required fields are shown

Title: Login blocked when no session location is selected
Preconditions: User has valid credentials; multiple login locations exist; no default location preselected
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username | 2. Enter a valid password | 3. Do not select a session location | 4. Click Log In
Expected_Result: The Log In action is disabled or rejected with a prompt to select a session location; no session is created

Title: Username field is case-insensitive while password is case-sensitive
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists with password Test1234!
Test_Steps: 1. Enter username in uppercase CLINICIAN1 | 2. Enter the exact-case password | 3. Select location | 4. Log In | 5. Log out | 6. Enter correct username with a wrong-case password | 7. Log In
Test_Data: Row1 Username=CLINICIAN1 Password=Test1234! => allow; Row2 Username=clinician1 Password=test1234! => deny
Expected_Result: Row1 authenticates successfully; Row2 is rejected, confirming password case sensitivity and username case insensitivity

Title: Leading and trailing whitespace in username is trimmed before authentication
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Enter username with leading and trailing spaces | 2. Enter the correct password | 3. Select location | 4. Log In
Expected_Result: Username is trimmed and authentication succeeds, or trimming is consistently applied per defined behavior without granting access to a different account

Title: Account is locked after the configured number of consecutive failed logins
Preconditions: Lockout threshold configured to 7 consecutive failures (OpenMRS allowedFailedLogins); user clinician1 exists; counter reset
Test_Steps: 1. Submit 7 consecutive logins with the correct username and a wrong password | 2. Submit an 8th login with the correct password
Test_Data: Username=clinician1; WrongPassword x7=Wrong#1..Wrong#7; then correct Password=Test1234!
Expected_Result: After reaching the threshold the account is locked; the subsequent attempt with the correct password is rejected with a lockout message, and the lockout event is auditable

Title: Boundary check one attempt below the lockout threshold still permits valid login
Preconditions: Lockout threshold = 7; user clinician1 exists; counter reset
Test_Steps: 1. Submit 6 failed logins with wrong password | 2. Submit a 7th login with the correct password
Expected_Result: The 7th attempt with the correct password succeeds because the threshold was not reached, and the failure counter resets on success

Title: Locked account auto-unlocks after the configured lockout interval elapses
Preconditions: Account clinician1 is in Locked state; lockout duration configured to 5 minutes
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm account is locked | 2. Wait for the configured lockout interval to elapse | 3. Attempt login with correct credentials
Expected_Result: After the lockout interval the account transitions from Locked back to Active and the valid login succeeds

Title: Failed-login counter resets after a successful authentication
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists; threshold = 7; counter reset
Test_Steps: 1. Submit 3 failed logins | 2. Submit 1 successful login | 3. Log out | 4. Submit 5 more failed logins | 5. Submit 1 login with correct password
Expected_Result: After the successful login the counter resets, so the later 5 failures do not trigger lockout and the final correct login succeeds

Title: Lockout enforcement is per-account and does not lock unrelated users
Preconditions: Users clinician1 and nurse1 both exist; threshold = 7
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger lockout on clinician1 with repeated wrong passwords | 2. Log in as nurse1 with valid credentials
Expected_Result: nurse1 logs in successfully, confirming lockout isolation per account

Title: User can log out and the session is terminated
Preconditions: User clinician1 is logged in at Outpatient Clinic
Test_Steps: 1. Click the user menu | 2. Click Log Out | 3. Observe redirect
Expected_Result: User is logged out, redirected to the login page, and the server-side session is invalidated

Title: After logout the browser Back button does not expose authenticated pages
Preconditions: User clinician1 is logged in and has viewed a patient dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient dashboard | 2. Log out | 3. Press the browser Back button | 4. Observe the page
Expected_Result: Protected content is not rendered from cache; the user is redirected to the login page and cannot view PHI without re-authenticating

Title: Reusing a session cookie captured before logout is rejected
Preconditions: User clinician1 logged in; the JSESSIONID cookie value has been captured
Test_Steps: 1. Capture the session cookie while logged in | 2. Log out via UI | 3. Replay an authenticated request using the captured cookie
Expected_Result: The replayed request is unauthenticated and rejected; the server-side session was invalidated on logout

Title: Idle session expires after the configured inactivity timeout
Preconditions: Inactivity timeout configured (e.g. 30 minutes); user clinician1 logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Leave the session idle past the configured timeout | 3. Attempt a protected action such as opening a patient record
Expected_Result: The session is expired; the protected action is blocked and the user is redirected to the login page requiring re-authentication

Title: Active user activity keeps the session alive before timeout
Preconditions: Inactivity timeout configured to 30 minutes; user clinician1 logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Perform a protected action every few minutes for longer than the timeout window | 3. Continue working
Expected_Result: The session remains valid because each activity refreshes the idle timer and no forced logout occurs

Title: Boundary check at exactly the configured timeout value
Preconditions: Inactivity timeout configured to exactly 30 minutes; user logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Remain idle for exactly the configured timeout minus a small margin then act | 3. Repeat remaining idle just past the timeout then act
Test_Data: Username=clinician1; Idle just-under and just-over 30 minutes
Expected_Result: Just under the threshold the session is still valid; just over the threshold the session is expired, confirming the boundary

Title: Same user can hold concurrent sessions in two different browsers
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists; two independent browsers available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as clinician1 in Browser A | 2. Log in as clinician1 in Browser B | 3. Perform an action in each
Expected_Result: Both sessions authenticate independently and each maintains its own session location and context per configured concurrency policy

Title: Logging out of one session does not terminate the other concurrent session
Preconditions: User clinician1 is logged in concurrently in Browser A and Browser B
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm both sessions active | 2. Log out in Browser A | 3. Perform a protected action in Browser B
Expected_Result: Browser A is logged out while Browser B remains authenticated and can continue working

Title: Concurrent sessions can operate at different session locations independently
Preconditions: User clinician1 has access to multiple login locations
Test_Steps: 1. Log in Browser A at Outpatient Clinic | 2. Log in Browser B at Inpatient Ward | 3. Capture each session location | 4. Create an activity in each
Test_Data: Browser A Location=Outpatient Clinic; Browser B Location=Inpatient Ward
Expected_Result: Each session retains its own selected location and activity is attributed to the correct location without cross-contamination

Title: User switches the active session location after login
Preconditions: User clinician1 logged in at Outpatient Clinic with access to Inpatient Ward
Test_Steps: 1. Open the session location control | 2. Select Inpatient Ward | 3. Confirm the change | 4. Open a new encounter
Expected_Result: The active session location updates to Inpatient Ward and new encounters are recorded against that location

Title: User cannot select a login location they are not authorized for
Preconditions: User clinician1 is restricted to Outpatient Clinic only
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Open the location selector | 3. Inspect the available locations
Expected_Result: Only authorized login locations appear; the restricted Inpatient Ward is not selectable and cannot be set as session location

Title: Remembered login location is preselected on the next login from the same browser
Preconditions: User clinician1 previously logged in at Outpatient Clinic with the remember-location option enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Log in selecting Outpatient Clinic and enable remember location | 2. Log out | 3. Return to the login page in the same browser
Expected_Result: On the next visit the login page preselects Outpatient Clinic as the default session location

Title: Remembered location is not applied in a different browser or after cookie clear
Preconditions: User clinician1 enabled remember location in Browser A
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm remembered location in Browser A | 2. Open the login page in Browser B with no prior cookies | 3. Observe location selector
Test_Data: Browser B has no remember cookie
Expected_Result: Browser B does not preselect a remembered location and requires explicit location selection

Title: Unauthenticated deep link redirects to login and returns to the target after authentication
Preconditions: User is not authenticated; a deep link to a patient dashboard URL is known
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deep-link URL while logged out | 2. Observe the redirect | 3. Authenticate with valid credentials and select location | 4. Observe the landing page
Expected_Result: The unauthenticated request redirects to the login page, and after successful login the user is returned to the originally requested patient dashboard

Title: Direct API call without authentication is rejected with 401
Preconditions: No active session; REST endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. Send a GET to a protected REST endpoint without any session cookie or auth header | 2. Inspect the HTTP status and body
Expected_Result: The server responds 401 Unauthorized and returns no PHI in the body

Title: Basic auth with valid credentials returns a session and authenticated flag
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists; REST API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET /ws/rest/v1/session with HTTP Basic credentials | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: Response 200 with authenticated true and the user and current session location in the payload

Title: Basic auth with wrong password is rejected
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists; REST API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET /ws/rest/v1/session with HTTP Basic credentials using a wrong password | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: Response 401 Unauthorized and authenticated is not true

Title: Session location can be set via the REST session endpoint
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session for clinician1; user authorized for Inpatient Ward
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate to obtain a session | 2. POST the sessionLocation uuid to /ws/rest/v1/session | 3. GET the session and verify
Expected_Result: The session location is updated to Inpatient Ward and the subsequent GET reflects the new location

Title: Logout via DELETE on the session endpoint invalidates the session
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session for clinician1 with a known cookie
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate and capture session | 2. Send DELETE /ws/rest/v1/session | 3. Replay a protected GET with the same cookie
Expected_Result: After DELETE the session is invalidated and the replayed protected request returns 401

Title: Password change rejects a value below minimum length
Preconditions: Password policy requires minimum length 8; user clinician1 logged in on the change password screen
Test_Steps: 1. Open Change Password | 2. Enter current password | 3. Enter a new password shorter than 8 characters | 4. Confirm and submit
Expected_Result: The change is rejected with a minimum-length validation message and the password is not updated

Title: Password change rejects a value missing required character classes
Preconditions: Password policy requires upper, lower, digit and non-digit; user logged in on change password screen
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt new password lacking a digit | 2. Attempt new password lacking an uppercase letter | 3. Attempt new password lacking a non-digit symbol
Test_Data: Row1 New=Password (no digit); Row2 New=test1234 (no upper); Row3 New=12345678 (no non-digit)
Expected_Result: Each non-compliant password is rejected with the specific complexity validation; only a fully compliant password would be accepted

Title: Password change accepts a value meeting all complexity rules
Preconditions: Password policy enforces complexity; user clinician1 logged in on change password screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter current password | 2. Enter a compliant new password | 3. Confirm the new password | 4. Submit | 5. Log out and log in with the new password
Expected_Result: The password is updated successfully and the user can authenticate with the new password

Title: Password change rejects when new password equals current password
Preconditions: Password policy prevents reuse of current password; user logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open Change Password | 2. Enter current password | 3. Enter the same value as the new password | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: The change is rejected with a message that the new password must differ from the current one

Title: Password change rejects when confirmation does not match
Preconditions: User clinician1 logged in on the change password screen
Test_Steps: 1. Enter current password | 2. Enter a compliant new password | 3. Enter a different confirmation value | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: The change is rejected with a passwords do not match validation and the password is unchanged

Title: Password reset requires re-authentication on next login
Preconditions: Administrator can reset a user password; user clinician1 exists
Test_Steps: 1. As admin reset clinician1 password | 2. Attempt login with the old password | 3. Attempt login with the new password
Expected_Result: The old password no longer works and only the newly set password authenticates successfully

Title: SQL injection strings in the login form do not authenticate or error the server
Preconditions: Login page available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an injection string in the username field | 2. Enter an injection string in the password field | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Login is rejected as invalid credentials, no SQL error is exposed, and no authentication bypass occurs

Title: Reflected script payload in username is neutralized and not executed
Preconditions: Login page available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a script payload as the username | 2. Enter any password | 3. Submit and observe the error rendering
Expected_Result: Login fails and the payload is rendered as inert text or rejected; no script executes in the browser

Title: Generic error message does not differentiate unknown user from wrong password
Preconditions: User clinician1 exists; user ghostuser does not exist
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt login for an existing user with a wrong password and record the message | 2. Attempt login for a non-existent user and record the message
Expected_Result: Both attempts return an identical generic error to prevent username enumeration

Title: Successful and failed login attempts are recorded for audit
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled; database access available; user clinician1 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Perform one successful login | 2. Perform one failed login | 3. Query the audit or login attempt records
Expected_Result: Both the successful and failed authentication events are persisted with user, timestamp, and outcome for compliance review

Title: Failed-login counter is persisted and queryable in the user store
Preconditions: Database access available; user clinician1 exists; counter reset
Test_Steps: 1. Submit 3 failed logins for clinician1 | 2. Query the user record for the failed-attempt count
Expected_Result: The persisted failed-login count for the user equals 3, confirming counter persistence used for lockout decisions

Title: Stored password is hashed and salted, never in plaintext
Preconditions: Database access available; user clinician1 exists with a known password
Test_Steps: 1. Query the stored password and salt columns for clinician1 | 2. Inspect the values
Expected_Result: The stored credential is a non-reversible salted hash; the plaintext password never appears in the database

Title: Login form fields and controls have accessible labels and names
Preconditions: Login page available; accessibility tooling or screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect username, password, location, and submit controls for programmatic labels | 2. Navigate the form with a screen reader
Expected_Result: All interactive controls expose accessible names and roles so assistive technology can identify and operate them

Title: Login form is fully operable by keyboard with visible focus order
Preconditions: Login page available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through username, password, location, and Log In | 2. Submit using the keyboard | 3. Observe focus indicators
Expected_Result: Focus moves in a logical order, each control shows a visible focus indicator, and login can be completed without a mouse

Title: Login error messages are announced to assistive technology
Preconditions: Login page available; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Submit invalid credentials | 2. Observe whether the error is announced via an accessible live region
Expected_Result: The invalid-credentials error is programmatically associated and announced so screen reader users are informed of the failure

Title: Login credentials are only submitted over HTTPS
Preconditions: Application served over TLS; ability to inspect network traffic
Test_Steps: 1. Open the login page | 2. Submit credentials | 3. Inspect the request scheme and any HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect
Expected_Result: Credentials are transmitted only over HTTPS; any HTTP access is redirected to HTTPS before credential entry

Title: Session cookie is marked HttpOnly and Secure
Preconditions: Application served over TLS; ability to inspect cookies
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Inspect the session cookie attributes in the browser or response headers
Expected_Result: The session cookie carries HttpOnly and Secure attributes, preventing script access and non-TLS transmission

Title: Pairwise combinations of credential validity, location selection, and account state
Preconditions: Users in Active and Locked states exist; multiple locations configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute each pairwise combination of credential validity, location selected vs none, and account state | 2. Record the outcome of each combination
Test_Data: P1 Valid+Location+Active=>login; P2 Valid+NoLocation+Active=>blocked; P3 Invalid+Location+Active=>rejected; P4 Valid+Location+Locked=>blocked
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the expected outcome, confirming the interaction of credentials, location, and account state

Title: Disabled (retired) user cannot authenticate
Preconditions: User olduser exists but is retired/disabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enter the disabled user credentials | 2. Select a location | 3. Log In
Expected_Result: Authentication is rejected because the account is disabled; no session is created

Title: User flagged for forced password change must change password before access
Preconditions: User clinician1 has the change-password-on-next-login flag set
Test_Steps: 1. Log in with valid credentials | 2. Observe the forced change-password prompt | 3. Attempt to navigate away without changing | 4. Set a compliant new password
Expected_Result: The user is forced to change the password before reaching protected pages; only after a compliant change is full access granted

Title: Exploratory probing of rapid repeated login submissions and double submit
Preconditions: Login page available; user clinician1 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly double-click Log In | 2. Submit several logins in quick succession | 3. Use the browser Back button mid-login | 4. Observe for duplicate sessions, errors, or counter anomalies
Expected_Result: No duplicate sessions or inconsistent failed-counter states arise; the system handles rapid and repeated submissions gracefully

Title: FHIR API rejects unauthenticated access to a Patient resource
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint exposed; no auth provided
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET to the FHIR Patient endpoint with no credentials | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: The server returns 401 and a FHIR OperationOutcome indicating security failure, with no patient data disclosed

Title: SMART on FHIR access token with insufficient scope is denied
Preconditions: SMART-enabled FHIR server; a token issued without patient/Patient.read scope
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain an access token lacking the required read scope | 2. Call the FHIR Patient read endpoint with that token | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: Access is denied with 403 and an OperationOutcome citing insufficient scope, enforcing least-privilege authorization

Title: Expired OAuth2 bearer token is rejected by the FHIR endpoint
Preconditions: SMART/OAuth2-enabled FHIR server; an expired bearer token available
Test_Steps: 1. Call the FHIR Patient endpoint using an expired bearer token | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: The request is rejected with 401 and an OperationOutcome; expired tokens grant no access

Title: HL7 v2 interface rejects a message from an unauthenticated or untrusted source
Preconditions: HL7 v2 MLLP listener configured with sending-application allowlist (Platform adapter)
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT message from a sending application not on the allowlist | 2. Observe the ACK
Expected_Result: The interface rejects the message with an application reject ACK (AR/AE) and does not process the untrusted feed

Title: Resource Adapter Layer maps a single login to the target platform credential store
Preconditions: Resource Adapter configured for omiiCARE target; user mapped across systems
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate once through the adapter | 2. Invoke a downstream protected resource on the omiiCARE platform | 3. Verify identity propagation
Expected_Result: The adapter authenticates the user and propagates a valid identity/token to the downstream platform without a second manual login

Title: Adapter denies access when downstream platform credential mapping is missing
Preconditions: Resource Adapter configured; user has no mapping to the omiiCARE target
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate through the adapter as an unmapped user | 2. Invoke a downstream omiiCARE resource | 3. Observe the result
Expected_Result: The adapter denies the downstream call due to the missing credential mapping and surfaces a clear authorization error

Title: Session identifier is rotated on successful authentication
Preconditions: Ability to capture the pre-login session id
Test_Steps: 1. Visit the login page and capture the anonymous session id | 2. Log in with valid credentials | 3. Capture the post-login session id and compare
Expected_Result: The session identifier changes after authentication, preventing session fixation attacks

Title: Optional single-session policy invalidates the older session on new login
Preconditions: Single-session-per-user policy enabled (Platform configuration); user clinician1 logged in Browser A
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm Browser A session active | 2. Log in as clinician1 in Browser B | 3. Perform a protected action in Browser A
Expected_Result: Under the single-session policy the older Browser A session is invalidated and forced to re-login when the new session starts

Title: Very long username input is handled without server error
Preconditions: Login page available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a 256-plus character username | 2. Enter any password | 3. Submit | 4. Observe handling
Expected_Result: The input is rejected as invalid credentials or validated by max length without an unhandled server error or truncation that grants access

Title: Logout is auditable with user and timestamp
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; database access; user clinician1 logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as clinician1 | 2. Log out | 3. Query audit records for the logout event
Expected_Result: A logout audit record exists with the user, timestamp, and event type for compliance traceability
